name: National Credit Care
description_short: Credit repair company that disputes inaccurate and unverifiable items on your credit reports with all three bureaus using FCRA-based processes. Based in Westminster, CO since ~2009.
description_long:
National Credit Care is a credit repair company headquartered in Westminster, Colorado, founded around 2009 and claiming approximately 17 years of industry experience as of 2026. The company operates entirely within the United States — no offshore outsourcing — and employs roughly 77 staff. Unlike HUD-approved housing counselors or NFCC-affiliated non-profit agencies, National Credit Care is a for-profit credit repair firm; no third-party certifications such as CDFI, HUD, or NFCC membership have been verified for this company.

The company's core service is FCRA-based credit dispute work: they review clients' credit reports from all three major bureaus (Equifax, Experian, TransUnion), identify items that are inaccurate, incomplete, misleading, or unverifiable, and submit formal dispute requests on the client's behalf. Bureaus are legally required under the Fair Credit Reporting Act to investigate disputed items and remove those they cannot verify within a mandated timeframe. National Credit Care frames this as a personalized process — they compare credit profiles to individual DNA, unique to each person. Additional offerings include a secured credit card product (available on a dedicated page of their website), a client-facing mobile app on both iOS and Android, an online client portal, and a separate lender portal for mortgage professionals.

What distinguishes National Credit Care in a crowded market is a notably high consumer satisfaction signal: a 4.6-star rating across 2,180 Google reviews as of early 2026. Their homepage explicitly targets homebuyers with the headline 'Let's get home loan ready,' positioning the service for consumers who need credit improvement before a mortgage application. The lack of long-term contracts is a genuine consumer-friendly feature — clients can cancel at any time. Their 100% US-based staffing claim is also a differentiator they actively market.

The honest picture includes real caveats. National Credit Care is not BBB accredited, and in June 2021 the BBB identified a formal pattern of complaints related to billing practices, collection activities, and customer service — a flag worth investigating before enrolling. No money-back guarantee was found on their website or in verified sources. Their pricing ($179 setup plus $89/month) sits at a moderate-to-premium level for the credit repair segment. Consumers should also understand that credit repair companies cannot legally guarantee specific outcomes; any negative item that is accurate and verifiable cannot be permanently removed.

pricing.guarantee_details: No money-back guarantee was identified on their website. Contact provider for current terms.

tiers.name: Credit Repair Service
features: FCRA-based dispute submissions to all three major bureaus
Direct credit bureau contact on client's behalf

pros: 4.6/5 Google rating from 2,180 reviews — among the stronger consumer satisfaction signals in the credit repair industry
Founded ~2009 with approximately 17 years of credit repair experience
No long-term contracts — clients can cancel at any time without penalty
100% US-based staff with no offshore outsourcing
Mobile app available on both iOS (App Store) and Android (Google Play)
Separate client portal and lender portal for mortgage professionals
Disputes submitted to all three major credit bureaus (Equifax, Experian, TransUnion)
cons: Not BBB accredited; BBB formally identified a pattern of complaints in June 2021 related to billing, collection activities, and customer service
$179 setup fee plus $89/month places it at a moderate-to-premium price point compared to some competitors
No money-back guarantee found on website or in verified third-party sources
No NFCC, HUD-approved, or non-profit status — this is a for-profit credit repair firm, not a free counseling resource
No publicly verified data on average results, typical timeline to dispute resolution, or success rates
best_for: Consumers preparing to apply for a home loan who need to address inaccurate or unverifiable negative items on their credit reports
People with documented errors, fraudulent accounts, or unverifiable derogatory marks across multiple credit bureaus
Clients who want US-based support and prefer managing the process via a mobile app or online portal
Borrowers who want flexibility with no long-term contract commitment
diagnosis: National Credit Care is best suited for consumers who want a professional, US-based team to handle FCRA dispute work across all three credit bureaus — particularly those gearing up for a home purchase. Their strong Google review volume is a real positive signal, but the BBB's 2021 pattern-of-complaints finding around billing practices means prospective clients should carefully review the fee structure and cancellation policy before enrolling.
services: Credit report analysis and initial consultation
FCRA-based dispute submissions to Equifax, Experian, and TransUnion
Removal of inaccurate or incomplete items from credit reports
Removal of unverifiable negative items via bureau investigation process
Direct communication with credit bureaus on client's behalf
iOS and Android mobile app for client account management
Online client portal for account access and progress tracking
Lender portal for mortgage professionals working with clients
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
